tokens: Good morning, Charlie; it's nice to hear your voice.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Did you guys have a site handover about 20 minutes ago?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Pause) Okay; yes, I guess we did.
Why, did you get keying there?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, you...(Hears Tony) Well, you dropped the up-link in a big blast of static, and that's why I pegged out the EKG about 20 minutes ago.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: The docs over here say, yeah, you did.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: If you're ready for the crew status report (as per Surface checklist page 3-7), I'll give it to you.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You mean you got John to eat one of those
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: In fact, he was sleeping so great that I just woke him up just a second ago, as a matter of fact.
I couldn't stand it any longer.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Well, you're right on the timeline.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Houston, we're ready to copy our lift-off times.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We've got the block data for the LM lift-off, LM Timeline Book.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: This assumes GET update of 00:11:48, and Ken will be getting that update in about 2 hours.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Starting with 22: 116 plus 29 plus 51; 118 plus 28 plus 22; 120 plus 26 plus 55; 122 plus 25 plus 28; 124 plus 24 plus 00; 126 plus 22 plus 32; and that assumes the GET update of 11 minutes and 48 seconds for Casper.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And we have an update to your Lunar Surface Checklist.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay; I guess we were too quick last night (at
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Don't worry, we'd've (means "we would have") done that.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Under that T-ephemeris update, we'd like to have the note, (reads slowly) "T-ephemeris to be used only in the event of LGC/CMC clock sync is required." And for the registers: R1, 00011; R2, 13346; R3, 25621.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay; say R1 and R2 again.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay; note is "T-ephemeris to be used only in event of LGC/CMC clock sync." Rl, 00011; R2, 13346; R3, 25621.
And that's that we got loaded, or is that what we'd load if you wanted us to sync up?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay; that's what you'd have to load to sync up after they fix the CSM time.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay; and that's the end of that.
And I'll brief you on the traverse whenever you're ready.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Let John get on the comm.
It'll be a couple of minutes.
We're going into the eat period, and we'll give you a call while we're eating.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Did you work all night too, Tony?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I've been home and sacked out.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Go ahead with your EVA stuff.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Pause) Now these are the Backroom's best guess.

The Spook sampling may be compromised by that ray from South Ray that you described, and since we're mostly interested in local Cayley on EVA-1, you'll probably have to spend a little time trying to differentiate between secondaries coming in from South Ray and local material.
You described most of the (South) Ray material as being very angular.
One of the clues may be rounded blocks, for local material.
At Flag (Crater at CB/73 on EVA-I, III 1 of 3), again, you may have that same problem.

Since we couldn't see over there, we really couldn't tell; and we don't want you to feel bound to Plum Crater there at Flag; if there's another place on the rim of Flag where you're more likely to get local material, feel free to head for it.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: But the color of the ray material is a pretty good gouge (meaning "indicator"); it's all white.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: At least, I think it is.
That's the way it...(Stops to listen)
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: It sure looked all white at pitchover, there.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And I guess we'd like you to go ahead on a normal EVA-1 there, and not worry too much about the local blocks in the LM-ALSEP area.
We'll try to pick them up at the end of EVA-1 or at Station 10 (near the LM) in EVA-2.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And, again, on the traverse itself, we'll skip the TV getting out, since we won't have AC and all and the high gain.
And, so, you don't have to worry about deploying the TV and tripod; and we'll leave the TV for Charlie when he gets the LCRU out.
We will need a few more words during the ALSEP deploy or, correction, during the LRV deploy, since we won't be able to watch it.
And, our best guess on an ALSEP area is still sort of northwest of you there.
But it will be completely up to you; whatever looks best.
And again, the use of the UV camera, we'll...
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I guess we're going to have to put the UV a little closer to the vehicle because of the current...Probably won't make a lot of difference there.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And if you guys are all for it and everything, we're still trying to crowd in three EVAs.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: What do you mean, are we all for it?!
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Laughing) Well, I just thought I'd give you a chance to put your vote in.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I'm not for crowding 'em in, but let's do three of them!
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Please pass on to the ALFMED P.I.
Benson and L.S Pinsky) in the Science Room that you can see those light flashes on the lunar surface just like Translunar coast, and you can also see them in the lunar orbit.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, did you notice any change in frequency?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: They're about the same (on the surface) as the...They're less than that experiment we ran (on the way to the Moon), but they were about the same as they were in lunar orbit.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: They were less than in the experiment, but about the same as in orbit.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Yeah, and I think there was about...Let me say, I maybe saw 10 before I went to sleep, and I think I got to sleep pretty fast.
So, they aren't really too numerous, but you can see them.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We think we're down to 117:03 (in the checklist) now.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I say, we're down to 117 hours and 3 minutes (which is Step 10 in the handwritten alterations to page 3-8).
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We're going to go ahead and do it, with the understanding that when we get them donned and get the PLSS on, we'll depress (the cabin) and go ahead and get out, because we don't have a very good way to keep time in here.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We do have a procedure here.
We'd like for you to mess with your steerable antenna a little bit, there.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, do you want me to do that now?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Panel 16 ( CB(16) ), on the Comm, S-Band Antenna, closed.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: On the steerable manual controls, match the indicated angles.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay; the Track Mode to Slew.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I think it might be working, John.
I just got a jump on my needle here.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It's always been stuck on 12 before, and now it's on minus 75.
But (in the antenna shadow) it doesn't look like it's 75 out there.
Of course, you don't know with the pitch.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (To Houston) Okay; what do you want to do with it now, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We're working on another problem here.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (To Charlie, somewhat incredulous) Working another problem?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We'd like you to close Inverter 2 circuit breaker, and select Inverter 2.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (To John) That's what I was afraid of.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay; we'll press on with the procedure here.
We had the Track Mode to Slew, set the Pitch control knob fully counter-clockwise.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, now set both Pitch and Yaw knobs fully clockwise.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay; go to Track Mode, Slew.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Negative; either in pitch or yaw.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Look on your panel 16, and see if the S-Band Antenna circuit breaker's open or closed.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Long Pause) (Annoyed) What a bunch of bullshit!
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay; we're just going to back out of that procedure.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: It did something in yaw then, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And match the indicated angles; Track Mode switch, Slew.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Tony, with the Track Mode to Off, both indicators are minus 75
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Set your Pitch at 180 and the Yaw at minus 12.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And go through, and we'll pull the circuit breakers that you have set for this procedure.
That's on panel 11, S-Band Antenna, Closed.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Displays, open; and the Heaters, open.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Inverter 2 circuit breaker, open; and Inverter 2, Off.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, let's press on with your suits.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: What are the nominal angles for lock-on error and...on this attitude?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: The 180 and minus 12 we gave you should be good angles.
(Pause) And that's for photography, not for lockon.
We're going to have you take a picture of it when you get out.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: When Charlie moves the needles in yaw, he can get it to oscillate - plus or minus what?
(Pause) The antenna doesn't sound like it's moving.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We'd like you to press on with the EVA work.
If we mess with that S-band any more, we'll do after the EVA.
I don't know whether it's right or not, but it says that you won't be able to hear the yaw motor.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay; but Charlie is looking at it (meaning the antenna shadow) out the window, and it's not moving.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: There's a latching mechanism in the thing there that should have released when the thing was unstowed, and we're going to try to have you take a picture of it.
There's a lever that pokes out there, and we can tell from the picture whether that latch released.
And there should be a little bit of slop in there so...
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: ...maybe he could drive it a little bit.
transcript.speaker: England
